summary: Question 1.3 from Pure pairs IX asks whether, in any 3-blockade with no transversal triangle, there exist distinct blocks $B_i, B_j$ containing a pure pair of polynomial size in the blockade width. The authors note it is a variant of a Conlon–Fox–Sudakov conjecture, equivalent to the $k=3$ case of Theorem 1.4, and reduce it to a sparse case, but leave it open. No follow-up paper resolving the question was found in a broad web search through May 2026.

pure pairs transversal triangle blockade width epsilon W^c 2024 2025 graph theory
Scott Seymour Spirkl transversal pure pairs 2024 2025 arxiv cycles blockade
notes: No follow-up found. The conjecture is the k=3 triangle case of a family of results about transversal cycles in blockades; the k>=4 cases are handled by Theorem 1.4 of the same paper. The authors reduce Question 1.3 to the sparse case but do not resolve it.
